# UC-21.1.2 · Substation RTU Communication Failure

## Description

Silent RTU loss leaves operators blind to field conditions; rapid detection of polling gaps avoids delayed switching decisions and compliance exposure for unmetered assets.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Substation RTU Communication Failure to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Forward RTU poll logs from the SCADA front end or protocol gateway (DNP3/IEC 104) via Edge Hub syslog or file monitor. Map poll success, timeout, and RTU identifiers. Alert when no successful poll is seen for longer than the expected scan period or when consecutive failures exceed threshold. **Domain context:** RTU/IED communication is the field-to-control-room path in IEC 60870-5-104 and IEEE 1815 (DNP3); silent polling loss often precedes visibility gaps during storms or cyber events—compare `age_sec` to your scan table (e.g. 2–10 s for critical RTUs). **Splunk:** Normalize `poll_status` values (OK, TIMEOUT, etc.) to lowercase in transforms; use `latest()` carefully in dashboards—pair with `age_sec` so stale “OK” rows do not hide a dead RTU.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=scada` `sourcetype="scada:rtu"` (rtu_id, poll_status, response_ms, substation_id).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward RTU poll logs from the SCADA front end or protocol gateway (DNP3/IEC 104) via Edge Hub syslog or file monitor. Map poll success, timeout, and RTU identifiers. Alert when no successful poll is seen for longer than the expected scan period or when consecutive failures exceed threshold. **Domain context:** RTU/IED communication is the field-to-control-room path in IEC 60870-5-104 and IEEE 1815 (DNP3); silent polling loss often precedes visibility gaps during storms or cyber events—compare `…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=scada sourcetype="scada:rtu"
| stats latest(_time) as last_poll, latest(poll_status) as last_status, latest(response_ms) as last_ms by rtu_id, substation_id
| eval age_sec=now()-last_poll
| where lower(last_status)!="ok" OR last_ms>5000 OR age_sec>600
| table substation_id, rtu_id, last_status, last_ms, age_sec
```

Understanding this SPL

**Substation RTU Communication Failure** — Silent RTU loss leaves operators blind to field conditions; rapid detection of polling gaps avoids delayed switching decisions and compliance exposure for unmetered assets.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=scada` `sourcetype="scada:rtu"` (rtu_id, poll_status, response_ms, substation_id). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: scada; **sourcetype**: scada:rtu. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=scada, sourcetype="scada:rtu". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rtu_id, substation_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where lower(last_status)!="ok" OR last_ms>5000 OR age_sec>600` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Substation RTU Communication Failure**): table substation_id, rtu_id, last_status, last_ms, age_sec


Step 3 — Validate
Compare poll health, timestamps, and quality bits with the SCADA front-end and field tech tickets for the same RTU or site. Confirm `_time` matches the protocol scan time you expect, not just log receipt time.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (RTU × health), Timeline (poll failures), Table (silent RTUs), Line chart (response time trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=scada sourcetype="scada:rtu"
| stats latest(_time) as last_poll, latest(poll_status) as last_status, latest(response_ms) as last_ms by rtu_id, substation_id
| eval age_sec=now()-last_poll
| where lower(last_status)!="ok" OR last_ms>5000 OR age_sec>600
| table substation_id, rtu_id, last_status, last_ms, age_sec
```

## Visualization

Status grid (RTU × health), Timeline (poll failures), Table (silent RTUs), Line chart (response time trend).
